scenarios.opening_query: The apartment flippers next door work seven days a week with crews starting before sunrise.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The city's noise ordinance bans construction before 7 am and the permits explicitly condition hours — code enforcement has ignored nine documented complaints, and compelling enforcement is the move.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their permits condition the hours and the ordinance bans pre-7 am work — code enforcement has ignored nine complaints. Compel enforcement.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether permit conditions and ignored enforcement are the lever
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're inside legal hours — it's the dust, the blocked alley, and the porta-potty at our fence line; classic private nuisance.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're within legal hours — it's the dust, blocked alley, and the porta-potty at our fence.

scenarios.opening_query: Our neighbor's koi pond project cracked our shared retaining wall and he says 'prove it.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || Real Property > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The wall's ownership itself is disputed — his deed and mine describe it differently, and quieting who owns and maintains it decides everything including the repair.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The wall's ownership is itself disputed — our deeds describe it differently. Quieting that decides everything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether title ambiguity underlies the damage fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The wall is unambiguously mine per both deeds — his excavation cracked it, and the engineer's causation report makes it a damages case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The wall's unambiguously mine — his dig cracked it, and the engineer's report proves causation.

scenarios.opening_query: A neighbor kid's drone crashed through our greenhouse roof and his parents laughed it off.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || Family Law > Parental Responsibility
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the fourth incident — the parental-responsibility statute makes parents liable for a minor's willful damage up to a cap, and that statutory claim is the clean path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Fourth incident — the parental-responsibility statute puts this on the parents up to the cap. That's the clean path.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the statutory parental-liability route applies
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'kid' is 19 — an adult; the negligence claim runs against him directly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The 'kid' is 19 — the claim runs against him directly.

scenarios.opening_query: Every big rain, the new subdivision uphill sends a brown river through our pasture fence.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our deed carries an 1897 irrigation ditch right the subdivision filled in — restoring that recorded water right, not just the runoff complaint, is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our deed carries an 1897 ditch right the subdivision filled in — restoring that recorded right is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether recorded water rights are impaired
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No recorded rights involved — it's surface-water diversion from bad grading, a drainage nuisance claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No recorded rights — it's bad-grading runoff, a drainage claim.

scenarios.opening_query: Our neighbor rents his house to a rotating cast of 'wellness retreats' that chant at dawn in the yard.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our subdivision's CC&Rs ban commercial use outright — the retreats are paid events, and covenant enforcement through the HOA is the direct path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our CC&Rs ban commercial use — these are paid events, so covenant enforcement through the HOA is the path.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No HOA or covenants — it's the noise at dawn, and the nuisance route is what's left.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No HOA or covenants — the dawn noise is what's left.

scenarios.opening_query: The man behind us screams threats over the fence at my wife whenever I leave for work.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's beyond neighbor noise — he times it to my departures, has followed her to the store twice, and she's documented ten incidents; a stalking protective order for her is the need.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He times it to my departures and has followed her to the store — ten documented incidents. She needs the protective order.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether targeted following elevates to protective relief
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He screams at everyone equally — the mailman included; it's a nuisance-and-mediation situation, not targeting.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He screams at everyone, the mailman included — nuisance and mediation, not targeting.
